revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:猪ノ子古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Inoko Kofun | image = [[File:Inoko Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | alternate_name = Inoko Kofun{{Sfn|Inoko Kofun (Ancient Tomb)|1989}} / Inoko No. 1 Kofun | affiliation = | location = Shimokamo, Kamo Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture
(within the grounds of Egi Shrine) | coordinates = {{Coord|34.574736|N|133.341389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | iso_code = JP-34 | type = Unknown | area = | burial = {{ill|Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|ja|横口式石槨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | auxiliary_mounds = | built = {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | Interred_individual = | imperial_tomb = | historic_sites = Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site "Inoko Kofun" | cultural_assets = | notes = | map = Japan Hiroshima | type = | map_dot_label = Inoko
Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Inoko Kofun''' (猪ノ子古墳, いのここふん) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in Shimokamo, Kamo Town, Fukuyama City, Hiroshima Prefecture, within the grounds of {{ill|Egi Shrine|ja|江木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. It has been Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Overview == [[File:Egi-jinja (Fukyama), keidai.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Egi Shrine grounds}}{{small|The Inoko Kofun mound is visible in the background.}}]] Located in eastern Hiroshima Prefecture, Inoko Kofun is built on a sloping site overlooking the Kamba Plain's northern valley{{Sfn|Inoko Kofun (Ancient Tomb)|1989}}. The site underwent archaeological excavation in {{date|2014}} ({{era|Heisei|26}}). The mound is significantly eroded, and its original type is unclear{{Sfn|Inoko Kofun (Encyclopedia Nipponica)}}. Hypotheses about its type include a 14-meter diameter {{ill|circular Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/circular Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} or a 14-meter square {{ill|square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Another theory suggests it may have been a polygonal mound. A surrounding moat is evident. The burial structure is a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening to the south. Details about grave goods remain unknown. The construction period is estimated to be the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}} during the {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|qid=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}. Some theories narrow this to the third quarter of the 7th century{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Horizontal stone chambers like this are rare outside the Kinai region, but examples such as {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}}, both in Fukuyama City, suggest strong ties between local elites and the Kinai region{{Sfn|Inoko Kofun (Encyclopedia Nipponica)}}. The design of Inoko Kofun closely resembles those found in Kinai, indicating direct influence{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. In {{date|1950}} ({{era|Showa|25}}), the site was Historic Site1 a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. == Chronology == * {{date|1950}} ({{era|Showa|25}}): Designated a Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site. * {{date|2014}}–{{date|2019}} ({{era|Heisei|26}}–{{era|Reiwa|1}}): Range and content confirmation surveys were conducted, revealing the possibility of a polygonal type (reported in {{date|2021}} by the Fukuyama City Board of Education). == burial burial == The burial structure consists of a {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横口式石槨|1=Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber|qq=}} opening southward. Its area are as follows: * Stone chamber: Length approximately 2.8 meters, width 1.1 meters, height 0.95 meters * Passageway: Length approximately 3.8 meters, width 1.7 meters, height 1.25 meters The stone chamber is constructed from five granite cut stones typeing the back wall, two side walls, ceiling stone, and floor stone. The passageway is larger, composed of side walls and two ceiling stones. The passageway floor is approximately 30 centimeters lower than the chamber floor{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. Traces of plaster were found between the stones{{Sfn|Inoko Kofun (Ancient Tomb)|1989}}{{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. This stone chamber closely resembles examples from the {{ill|Koseyama Kofun Cluster|en|Koseyama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11479773|lt=Koseyama Kofun Cluster|1=Koseyama Kofun Cluster}} (Kinai 323rd Kofun, {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|qid=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}}) and {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun group|qq=}} (Hiraoyama 102nd Kofun, {{ill|Kashiwara|en|Kashiwara|qid=Q873583|lt=Kashiwara, Osaka|1=Kashiwara}}){{Sfn|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}. == Historic Site == === Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Inoko Kofun - Designated {{date|1950}} ({{era|Showa|25}})[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120530.html Inoko Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board).. == References == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == {{small|Sources used for article writing}} * Site explanation board * Encyclopedias ''' {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Tadahiko Makabe|chapter=Inoko Kofun|title={{ill|Encyclopedia Nipponica|en|Encyclopedia Nipponica|qid=Q2660112|lt=Encyclopedia Nipponica|1=Encyclopedia Nipponica}}|publisher=Shogakukan|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Inoko Kofun (Encyclopedia Nipponica)}}}} ''' {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=|chapter=Shimokamo Village|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213509}} 35 Hiroshima Prefecture names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1982|isbn=4582490352|ref={{Harvid|Shimokamo Village (Heibonsha)|1982}}}} ''' {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Takashi Kozu|chapter=Inoko Kofun|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}}|publisher=Tokyodo Publishing|year=1989|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Inoko Kofun (Ancient Tomb)|1989}}}} * Other ''' {{Cite book|language=Japanese|author=Shiraishi Taichiro|chapter=On Bingo's Horizontal Stone Chambers|title=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum Report 11|publisher=Osaka Prefectural Chikatsu Asuka Museum|year=2007|ref={{Harvid|Shiraishi Taichiro|2007}}}} == Related Reading == {{small|Not used for this article}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=Gibi Vol. 2 -On Hiroshima Prefectural Historic Site Inoko Kofun-|publisher=Gibi Tomo-no-Kai|year=1974|ref=}} * {{Cite book|language=Japanese|title=Hiroshima Prefectural Designated Inoko Kofun: Survey Report on Damage Range and Content (Fukuyama City Archaeological Report Vol. 47)|publisher=Fukuyama City Board of Education|year=2021|ref=}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No.1 Kofun|lt=Oichi No. 1 Kofun|ja|尾市1号墳|1=Oichi No.1 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No. 1 Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Inoko Kofun}} * [https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/bunkazai/bunkazai-data-206120530.html Inoko Kofun] (Hiroshima Prefectural Education Board) * [https://www.city.fukuyama.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/bunka/64155.html Inoko Kofun] (Fukuyama City Homepage) {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|猪ノ子古墳|version=103146403|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:猪ノ子古墳]] {{Commons category|Inoko Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q56347601}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Hiroshima prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
